# Make Your Own Slow Cooker Beef Bone Broth

<https://thehealthyfoodie.com/slow-cooker-beef-bone-broth/>

---

October 7, 2016

# How to Make Nutrient-Dense Slow Cooker Beef Bone Broth

[Jump to Recipe](#recipe) //

 [300 *Comments »*](https://thehealthyfoodie.com/slow-cooker-beef-bone-broth/#comments)

![Bone Broth is one of the most nutrient rich and powerful superfoods there is out there! Learn how to make your own in a slow cooker with very minimal efforts on your part!](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCAwIDAnPjwvc3ZnPg==)

*This easy slow cooker beef bone broth recipe transforms simple ingredients into a nutrient-packed superfood in 18-36 hours. Made with roasted beef bones, vegetables, and apple cider vinegar, this homemade bone broth delivers collagen, minerals, and amino acids that support gut health, immunity, and joint function. Perfect for sipping warm or using as a cooking base.*

![Big cup of golden-brown bone broth garnished with fresh herbs and freshly cracked pepper with spoon on its side. ](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCA2NDAgOTYwJz48L3N2Zz4=)

## Why This Slow Cooker Bone Broth Recipe Works

This recipe succeeds because it combines three critical elements: **proper bone preparation, extended cooking time, and acid addition.** Roasting the bones at 400°F develops **deep flavor compounds** through the Maillard reaction, while the 24-hour slow cooking process **breaks down collagen** into bioavailable gelatin. Apple cider vinegar helps **extract minerals from the bones**, creating a nutrient-dense broth that **gels when cooled**.

The slow cooker method maintains consistent low heat **without requiring constant monitoring**, making it accessible for busy home cooks. Unlike stovetop methods that risk boiling *(which can create cloudy broth)*, the slow cooker’s gentle heat produces clear, flavorful results every time.

![Clear glass jars filled with golden bone broth ](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCA2NDAgOTYwJz48L3N2Zz4=)

## Nutritional Benefits of Homemade Bone Broth

### **Collagen and Gelatin**:

Supports skin elasticity, joint health, and gut lining integrity. The slow cooking process converts tough collagen into easily digestible gelatin.

### **Essential Minerals**:

High in calcium, magnesium, phosphorus, and potassium—crucial for bone health and muscle function.

### **Amino Acids**:

Contains glycine (supports sleep and detoxification), proline (collagen synthesis), and glutamine (gut health).

### **Digestive Support**:

The gelatin content helps heal and seal the gut lining, potentially reducing inflammation and improving nutrient absorption.

### **Immune Function**:

Traditional remedy backed by modern research showing anti-inflammatory properties and immune system support.

![Cup of hot bone broth garnished with fresh parsley and black pepper, steam visible](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCA2NDAgOTYwJz48L3N2Zz4=)

## Key Ingredients for Perfect Bone Broth

### **Essential Ingredients:**

- **Mixed beef bones (3-4 lbs)**: Use oxtail, knuckle bones, neck bones, and short ribs for optimal collagen content. While beef is most common, **[chicken bones](https://thehealthyfoodie.com/dutch-oven-chicken-bone-broth/)** work equally well, as do bones from any other animal. Lamb bones produce an exceptionally fragrant broth worth trying.
- **Apple cider vinegar (2 tbsp)**: Draws minerals from bones without affecting taste
- **Aromatic vegetables**: Carrots, celery, and onions add depth of flavor and natural sweetness to balance the rich bone flavor *(keep peels for enhanced color and nutrients)*
- **Bay leaf**: Adds subtle herbaceous notes
- **Filtered water**: Fills 7-quart slow cooker completely

### **Quality Matters**:

Choose grass-fed, pasture-raised bones when possible. CAFO-raised animals often produce broth that won’t gel properly and may contain unwanted additives.

---

## Recipe Overview

**Prep Time**: 20 minutes  
**Cook Time**: 18-36 hours  
**Total Time**: 18+ hours  
**Yield**: 4 cups concentrated broth  
**Difficulty**: Easy  
**Equipment**: 7-quart slow cooker, roasting pan, fine-mesh strainer

![Raw mixed beef bones including oxtail and knuckle bones arranged on a white baking sheet before roasting](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCA2NDAgOTYwJz48L3N2Zz4=)

## How to Make Slow Cooker Bone Broth: Step-by-Step

### Step 1: Roast the Bones

Preheat oven to 400°F. Arrange the bones in a single layer on a rimmed baking sheet.

Optionally, you can season the bones with salt and pepper before roasting, although saving this for later allows for better control, depending on what you intend to use the broth for.

![Golden-brown roasted beef bones on baking sheet after 60 minutes in 400°F oven, showing caramelized edges](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCA2NDAgOTYwJz48L3N2Zz4=)

**Roast for 60 minutes**, flipping each bone over half way through cooking time. This step is non-negotiable, as it will confer a beautiful golden color to the bones and fill them with TONS of flavor, both of which will later get passed down to the broth…

![Roughly chopped carrots, celery, and onions with peels in 6-quart slow cooker with bay leaf](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCA2NDAgOTYwJz48L3N2Zz4=)

### Step 2: Prepare the Vegetables

Roughly chop the carrots, celery, and onions. Don’t bother peeling the vegetables—the skins will add nutrients and color to the broth. Place everything in a **[7 quart slow cooker](https://amzn.to/2Ms6tRU)** along with the bay leaf and apple cider vinegar.

![Golden-brown roasted beef bones being added to slow cooker with celery and carrots](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCA2NDAgOTYwJz48L3N2Zz4=)

### Step 3: Combine and Fill

Add the hot roasted bones directly to the slow cooker. Fill completely with filtered water, until the bones are fully submerged.

![Water being poured over roasted bones placed in slow cooker with vegetables](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCA2NDAgOTYwJz48L3N2Zz4=)

The broth will simmer gently rather than boil vigorously, so there’s no risk of overflow. Just leave about an inch at the top for safety.

![Roasted bones added to slow cooker with vegetables, covered completely with water](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCA2NDAgOTYwJz48L3N2Zz4=)

### Step 4: Slow Cook Low and Long

Set the slow cooker to LOW, cover, and cook for 18-36 hours until the broth reaches a rich brown color and fills your kitchen with an incredible aroma. A 24-hour simmering time is recommended to extract maximum nutrients from the bones, though 18 hours will still produce excellent results if time is limited.

![Deep brown bone broth simmering in slow cooker after 24 hours of cooking, showing rich color](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCA2NDAgOTYwJz48L3N2Zz4=)

### Step 5: Strain and Store

Once the broth is done to your liking, carefully remove the larger pieces with a slotted spoon and place them in a strainer set over a large bowl to collect the excess broth. Press them down to extract all the liquid they contain.

![Straining finished bone broth through fine-mesh sieve into large stainless steel bowl](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCA2NDAgOTYwJz48L3N2Zz4=)

Strain the remaining broth through a fine-mesh sieve or colander lined with cheesecloth to catch even the smallest debris.

![Clear glass jars filled with golden bone broth showing proper gel consistency when cooled](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCA2NDAgOTYwJz48L3N2Zz4=)

Finally, transfer your bone broth into individual jars and refrigerate promptly.

The fat will float to the surface and, when chilled, form a protective barrier that prevents air contact with your broth. Once cooled, you can remove some or all of this fat layer and save it for cooking—it’s incredibly flavorful and excellent for sautéing vegetables or roasting.

![Clear glass jars filled with golden bone broth showing proper gel consistency when cooled](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCA2NDAgOTYwJz48L3N2Zz4=)

## Serving Recommendations

**As a Beverage**: Heat 1 cup broth, season with sea salt, black pepper, and fresh herbs. Sip warm for comfort and nutrition.

**Daily Serving**: 1 cup provides optimal benefits without overwhelming sodium intake.

### **Cooking Applications**:

- Use as base for soups, stews, and gravies
- Cook grains and legumes in broth instead of water
- Add to stir-fries for umami depth
- Freeze in ice cube trays for easy portioning

![Cup of hot bone broth garnished with fresh parsley and black pepper, steam visible](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCA2NDAgOTYwJz48L3N2Zz4=)

## Expert Tips for Bone Broth Success

1. **Don’t Skip Roasting**: This step is essential for flavor development
2. **Use Bone Variety**: Mix marrow bones with joint bones for best texture
3. **Add Acid Early**: Vinegar needs time to work on extracting minerals
4. **Keep Temperature Low**: High heat creates cloudy, less nutritious broth
5. **Strain Twice**: First through colander, then fine-mesh sieve for clarity
6. **Cool Quickly**: Refrigerate within 2 hours to prevent bacterial growth
7. **Save the Fat**: The rendered fat layer is excellent for cooking

![Cup of hot bone broth garnished with fresh parsley and black pepper, steam visible](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCA2NDAgOTYwJz48L3N2Zz4=)

## Recipe Variations and Adaptations

### Chicken Bone Broth

Replace beef bones with 3 lbs chicken carcasses, wings, and necks. Reduce cooking time to 12-24 hours.

### Lamb Bone Broth

Substitute lamb bones for uniquely fragrant, mineral-rich broth. Same method and timing.

### Vegetable Add-Ins

- Mushrooms for umami
- Ginger and turmeric for anti-inflammatory benefits
- Parsley stems for additional minerals

### Seasoning Variations

Add during final reheating:

- **Asian:** Ginger, scallions, miso paste
- **Mexican:** Cilantro, lime, jalapeño
- **Mediterranean:** Rosemary, thyme, lemon

---

## Nutritional Benefits of Homemade Bone Broth

**Collagen and Gelatin**: Supports skin elasticity, joint health, and gut lining integrity. The slow cooking process converts tough collagen into easily digestible gelatin.

**Essential Minerals**: High in calcium, magnesium, phosphorus, and potassium—crucial for bone health and muscle function.

**Amino Acids**: Contains glycine (supports sleep and detoxification), proline (collagen synthesis), and glutamine (gut health).

**Digestive Support**: The gelatin content helps heal and seal the gut lining, potentially reducing inflammation and improving nutrient absorption.

**Immune Function**: Traditional remedy backed by modern research showing anti-inflammatory properties and immune system support.

---

## Troubleshooting Common Bone Broth Issues

**Problem: Broth doesn’t gel when cooled**  
**Solution**: Use more joint bones (knuckle, neck), cook longer, or add chicken feet for extra collagen

**Problem**: **Cloudy broth**  
**Solution**: Keep temperature low, don’t boil, and ensure proper straining

**Problem**: **Weak flavor**  
**Solution**: Always roast bones first, use proper bone-to-water ratio, cook minimum 18 hours

**Problem**: **Too salty**  
**Solution**: Don’t add salt during cooking; season individual servings instead

**Problem**: **Greasy texture**  
**Solution**: Refrigerate and remove solidified fat layer before reheating

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### **Q: How long does bone broth last in the refrigerator?**

A: Properly stored bone broth lasts 5-7 days refrigerated or up to 6 months frozen.

### **Q: Can I reuse bones for a second batch?**

A: No, after 18-36 hours of cooking, the bones have released all their collagen, minerals, and nutrients. A second batch would yield minimal nutritional value and weak flavor. It’s better to start fresh with new bones.

### **Q: Is it safe to cook bone broth for 24+ hours?**

A: Yes, when kept at proper temperature (below 200°F) in a slow cooker designed for extended cooking.

### **Q: Why add apple cider vinegar?**

A: The acid helps extract minerals from bones without affecting taste. You won’t taste it in the final product.

### **Q: Can I make bone broth without roasting the bones?**

A: While possible, roasting is essential for proper flavor and color development.

### **Q: What’s the difference between bone broth and regular stock?**

A: Bone broth cooks longer (18+ hours vs 4-6 hours), uses more bones, and focuses on extracting maximum nutrients.

---

## Bottom Line

This slow cooker bone broth recipe delivers restaurant-quality results with minimal hands-on effort. The extended cooking time and proper technique **extract maximum nutrition from bones**, creating a **versatile superfood that supports overall health.** While it requires patience, the active preparation time is minimal, making it perfect for meal prep enthusiasts.

The key to success lies in **quality ingredients, proper bone roasting, and adequate cooking time**. Don’t rush the process—the longer cook time is what transforms simple bones into liquid gold **packed with collagen, minerals, and amino acids.**

Whether you’re sipping it for comfort, using it as a cooking base, or incorporating it into a healing diet, this bone broth recipe provides an **accessible way to add serious nutrition to your routine.**

![Bone Broth is one of the most nutrient rich and powerful superfoods there is out there! Learn how to make your own in a slow cooker with very minimal efforts on your part!](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0naHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmcnIHZpZXdCb3g9JzAgMCAxNTAgMTUwJz48L3N2Zz4=)

4.77 from 96 votes

## Slow Cooker Beef Bone Broth

Prep Time: 20 minutes minutes

Cook Time: 1 day day

Total Time: 1 day day 20 minutes minutes

Transform roasted beef bones into nutrient-rich broth packed with collagen, minerals, and amino acids. Perfect for sipping or cooking.

Servings: 4 cups

## Ingredients

[US Customary](#) – [Metric](#)

- 3-4 lbs of mixed beef bones, oxtail, knuckles, neckbones and/or short ribs
- 2 medium carrots
- 3 celery stalks
- 2 medium onions
- 2 tablespoons [apple cider vinegar](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B007Y6P90E/ref=as_li_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=390957&creativeASIN=B007Y6P90E&linkCode=as2&tag=theheafoo03-20)
- 1 bay leaf
- 10-12 cups water

### Equipment

- [7-Quart Slow Cooker](https://amzn.to/2Ms6tRU)

- [Rimmed Baking Sheet](https://amzn.to/3Tf1xmY)

- [Fine Mesh Sieve](https://amzn.to/3ZVmKWR)

## Instructions

- Preheat oven to 400°F.

- Place the bones in a single layer on a baking sheet or roasting pan; roast for 60 minutes, flipping each bone over half way through cooking time.

- Place chopped vegetables (don't even bother peeling them), bay leaf, and vinegar in 7-quart slow cooker. Add roasted bones and cover completely with water.

- Cook on LOW for 18-36 hours, until deep brown and aromatic.

- Carefully remove the larger pieces with a slotted spoon and place them in a colander set over a large bowl to collect the excess broth. Then, strain the broth through a [fine meshed sieve](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B007TUQF9O/ref=as_li_ss_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=390957&creativeASIN=B007TUQF9O&linkCode=as2&tag=theheafoo03-20), or through a strainer that's been lined with [cheesecloth](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00JS70WFY/ref=as_li_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=390957&creativeASIN=B00JS70WFY&linkCode=as2&tag=theheafoo03-20).

- Transfer the bone broth into jars and refrigerate promptly. When cooled, remove fat layer and save it for cooking.

- Reheat before serving and season to taste.

### Notes

- Don’t peel vegetables—skins add nutrients and color
- Save the rendered fat layer for cooking; it’s incredibly flavorful
- 7-quart slow cooker essential for proper water coverage
- Properly made broth will gel when refrigerated
- Store up to 7 days refrigerated or 6 months frozen
- For clearest broth, strain twice through fine-mesh sieve

### Nutrition

Serving: 1cup, Calories: 41kcal, Carbohydrates: 9g, Protein: 1g, Fat: 1g, Saturated Fat: 1g, Sodium: 79mg, Potassium: 256mg, Fiber: 2g, Sugar: 4g, Vitamin A: 5230IU, Vitamin C: 7mg, Calcium: 53mg, Iron: 1mg

Course: Condiment

Cuisine: American
